title: Aureline local-safe baseline record
description: Boundary schema for local_safe_baseline_record, the inspectable list of launch-critical local-first workflows (editing, save, search, Git, export, diagnostics, build/test/debug, undo, open-recent, local docs inspect, cached provider snapshot inspect) that a deployment claims are usable now. The failover-continuity banner cites this record so outage, maintenance, migration, or connectivity-degrade copy can name what still works locally rather than only naming the broken service. The baseline mints no new deployment-profile, control-plane-service, or data-plane-capability vocabulary; it composes the launch_critical_workflow_class set with the deployment_profile_class re-export and the local_core_continuity_packet refs already frozen in /docs/deployment/locality_and_continuity_seed.md and /schemas/deployment/local_core_continuity_packet.schema.json. Raw URLs, raw hostnames, raw tenant names, raw account ids, raw endpoint credentials, raw policy bodies, and raw secret material never cross this boundary; the record carries opaque refs and redaction-aware sentences only.

schema_version.description: Schema version for this baseline record. Additive-minor changes bump this integer.

workflow_rows.description: One row per launch-critical local-first workflow the baseline claims. The workflow_class set is closed; surfaces MAY NOT invent rows outside it.

__fixture__.description: Optional fixture annotation block. Never part of the runtime record.

stable_token.description: Opaque stable token. Raw URLs, raw hostnames, raw tenant names, raw account ids, raw endpoint credentials, raw policy bodies, and raw secret material never appear here.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short redaction-aware sentence safe for UI, support export, and admin review surfaces.

baseline_kind_class.description: Closed baseline-kind vocabulary. A baseline cannot be unspecified; the kind frames what the rows are claiming.

launch_critical_workflow_class.description: Closed launch-critical workflow vocabulary. The list is intentionally narrow: only workflows that MUST keep working when the user opens Aureline in a degraded environment.

availability_class.description: Re-export of the data-plane availability vocabulary already frozen in the locality / continuity seed plus an explicit available_with_freshness_label state for cached reads.

freshness_posture_class.description: Re-export of the freshness vocabulary in the local-core continuity packet.

composes_with.description: Opaque back-references to upstream records this baseline composes against. The baseline does not mint parallel locality, profile, or connectivity vocabulary; consumers cross-walk via these refs.
